explanation: This command uses standard syntax and matches the Sigma rule by exporting a registry path associated with PuTTY. It is a direct test case for the rule.
This command omits quotes around the registry path, which is still valid in many Windows environments. It tests whether the rule is sensitive to quoted vs. unquoted paths.
This command adds extra spaces between arguments to test whether the rule is sensitive to spacing variations in the command line.
This command adds an unnecessary /v flag (common in other Windows tools) to test whether the rule is sensitive to irrelevant flags in the command line.
This command uses the most standard and minimal form of the command to test the baseline detection capability of the Sigma rule.
